data.narrative:
Blue Water Autonomy is a Series A-stage autonomous systems company in the Defense & Security sector, founded by Rylan Hamilton, Austin Gray, and Scott N. Miller. The valuation analysis draws on a peer universe of 10 comparable companies—2 publicly traded (DRSHF, IDEAFORGE.NS) and 8 private firms including Stark Defence, Vatn Systems, Ares Industries, Anduril Industries, and Scale AI.

Two valuation methods were applied. Stage-progression analysis uses the company's $50M total raised as a proxy for current value and applies a 2.0x multiplier typical of Series A-to-next-stage advancement, yielding $100M. Peer-median analysis examines three same-sector, same-stage comparables: Stark Defence ($500M), Vatn Systems ($300M), and Ares Industries ($150M), producing a median valuation of $300M and a range of $150M–$500M. The dispersion within the peer set reflects heterogeneity in customer traction and defense-sector positioning at this stage.

Triangulating both methods produces a valuation range of $85M (low) to $345M (high), with a median of $200M. The wide spread reflects uncertainty inherent to Series A defense-tech valuations, where customer concentration, contract backlog, and regulatory pathway materially affect comparability. Key unknowns include Blue Water Autonomy's specific revenue run-rate relative to peers and the maturity of its go-to-market motion within defense procurement channels.
